behaviors.description: Primary translator produces grammatically coherent translation preserving meaning, not word-for-word substitution
behaviors.justification: Grammatical coherence and meaning preservation can only be verified by a real neural machine translation model. A mocked translator would always return 'correct' output, hiding real failures where the API returns incoherent, meaningless, or nonsensical translations. No amount of mock configuration can substitute for actual model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real NMT API with diverse source texts (including idiomatic language), verify output preserves semantic meaning rather than word-for-word substitution, optionally against reference translations or BLEU-style metrics.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: At Agentic level, we reliably catch bad model outputs because we're calling the real NMT model. Cannot be missed.

behaviors.description: When translation API returns 503 error, service transparently falls back without returning error to caller
behaviors.justification: Requires real HTTP error handling + real fallback wiring together. Can use a mock/test HTTP server returning 503 rather than calling real API (System overkill), but mocking the HTTP client entirely would hide real error-handling bugs in the client library. Need real HTTP client code path + real SQLite fallback logic exercised together on error.
behaviors.test_description: Set up test HTTP server returning 503, real SQLite with fallback phrases, send translation request, verify: (1) no error returned to caller, (2) fallback translation returned instead, (3) request completes successfully.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration catches if service returns 503 to caller, fails to fallback, or fallback returns wrong data. Real HTTP error-handling behavior is verified.

behaviors.description: Dictionary-based fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases in SQLite database
behaviors.justification: Must use real SQLite database and real query execution. Mocking the database hides real failures: SQL syntax bugs, encoding issues, data corruption, or index failures. The behavior is specifically about correct retrieval from persistent storage—cannot be tested in isolation without real I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Populate real SQLite database with known phrase pairs (diverse languages, special characters), call fallback translator with existing and non-existing phrases, verify correct translations returned for existing phrases and graceful handling for missing ones.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration with real SQLite catches SQL bugs, encoding issues, and data persistence failures. No critical failures missed.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores metric
behaviors.justification: Requires both real edit distance computation and real metric storage. Mocking the database hides persistence bugs; mocking the computation hides algorithm bugs. The behavior involves storing data in a real database—integration-level I/O is necessary to catch storage/indexing/encoding failures.
behaviors.test_description: Generate pairs of primary/fallback translations with known edit distances (hardcoded reference distances), call quality tracking, verify: (1) edit distance computed correctly, (2) metric persisted in real database, (3) metric retrievable with correct values.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration catches both algorithm and persistence bugs. Mocking either part would hide real failures.

behaviors.description: When both translators available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in background
behaviors.justification: The selection logic (return primary, not fallback) can be tested at Integration by mocking both translators with controlled outputs. The behavior fails if service returns fallback or doesn't log. Integration level catches the core failure: wrong translator selected. However, 'in the background' implies async/non-blocking—mocking logging hides real failures if logging blocks the response or fails.
behaviors.test_description: Mock primary translator returning known string A, mock fallback returning known string B, mock logging, send request, verify: (1) response contains primary translation A, (2) quality comparison log was called. Optionally test with real async logging to catch blocking issues.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration tests selection logic but misses real async logging failures. If 'background' logging is critical to the behavior (must not block response), use System level with real async logging to catch blocking bugs.
